name: Ethiopian Civil War / Derg / 1983-85 famine
summary: The Ethiopian Civil War during the Derg/PDRE period was a multi-front war involving the Ethiopian state against Eritrean, Tigrayan/EPRDF, Oromo, and other insurgencies, alongside severe urban repression and counterinsurgency campaigns. The 1983-85 famine was not simply a drought disaster: Human Rights Watch/Africa attributed a large share of famine mortality to government counterinsurgency, food restrictions, forced relocation, and misuse of relief. Conservative documented roll-up suggests roughly 700,000-940,000 deaths, while broader secondary estimates reach 1-2 million but are not cleanly decomposed.

military_killed.notes: HRW/Africa states that at least 100,000 soldiers were killed in the wars, but does not fully split those deaths by side. Most large battlefield losses described in the sources were Ethiopian government soldiers/conscripts; the figure is used as a conservative all-sides military floor, not a precise Derg-only total.

breakdown.cause: Red Terror executions, disappearances, torture deaths
breakdown.victims_group: students, academics, EPRP/MEISON members, suspected opponents, urban civilians
breakdown.notes: HRW/Africa gives at least 10,000 killed in Addis Ababa alone during the Red Terror; a later HRW dispatch says over 150,000 students, academics, and political opponents were killed during the Red Terror.

breakdown.cause: bombing, shelling, massacres, shootings, summary execution
breakdown.victims_group: rural civilians in Eritrea, Tigray, Ogaden, Wollo, Gojjam, Gonder, Shewa and other war zones
breakdown.notes: HRW/Africa states that direct killing and wounding of civilians by the Ethiopian army and air force undoubtedly exceeded 150,000, excluding famine and resettlement.
Civilian casualties in the Ogaden alone during the year following the Somali defeat were estimated at 25,000.

breakdown.victims_group: civilians at Hawzen/Hausien market town, Tigray
breakdown.notes: HRW sources give 1,800 in Evil Days and about 2,500 in Reckoning Under the Law for the June 1988 bombing.

breakdown.victims_group: civilians at Sheib and nearby Eritrean villages
breakdown.notes: At Sheib about 80 civilians were crushed by tanks and 320 killed by gunfire; at least 100 more civilians were killed in nearby villages.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The 150,000 figure is the best conservative floor for direct civilian killing by the Derg state. Named incidents do not sum to the total because many massacres and bombings were never fully documented.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: famine-affected civilians, especially in Tigray, Wollo, Eritrea, northern Shewa and other war-affected regions
deaths_from_actions.notes: HRW/Africa estimated total famine deaths in 1983-85 at 400,000-590,000 and attributed 225,000-317,000 to government human-rights violations and counterinsurgency policies.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: forcibly relocated peasants and famine victims
deaths_from_actions.notes: Evil Days gives at least 50,000 deaths from resettlement; Reckoning Under the Law says about 600,000 were forcibly relocated and perhaps 100,000 died.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: civilians in and around besieged Asmara
deaths_from_actions.notes: HRW identified military requisitioning and blockade as creating severe hunger in Asmara in 1990-91, but no defensible separate mortality estimate was found.

sides.name: EPLF / Eritrean insurgent side
members: Eritrean rebel forces in the EPLF-led dyad

military_killed.notes: PRIF/UCDP gives 98,927 fatalities from fighting between Eritrean rebels and the Ethiopian government, and cites another estimate of 200,000 for the Eritrean secession war, but these are all-sides/all-persons dyad totals and cannot be converted into EPLF combatant deaths.

breakdown.notes: One driver was killed in the EPLF attack on a 34-truck convoy south of Asmara in October 1987.

breakdown.victims_group: alleged collaborators, WPE members, informers, prison officials
breakdown.notes: HRW reports EPLF assassinations of alleged collaborators, about one announced assassination every two weeks in late 1990 and early 1991, but no total death count is provided.

breakdown.victims_group: Afar men resisting EPLF conscription at Asela/Danakil
breakdown.notes: Reports include about 20 casualties and an official Afar claim of 300 fatalities; HRW judged total dead and wounded unlikely to exceed 250, but does not provide a deaths-only figure.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: EPLF abuses were documented, but available sources do not support a responsible aggregate civilian-death total.

military_killed.notes: PRIF/UCDP gives 53,483 deaths for the TPLF/EPDM/EPRDF vs government dyad, including 484 one-sided government-violence victims, but does not split deaths by side or combatant/civilian status.

breakdown.notes: Two World Vision employees were killed and four wounded in the TPLF/EPDM attack on Alamata in March 1986; HRW cites witnesses saying the killings were deliberate.

breakdown.cause: kidnapping/attacks on relief centers and routes
breakdown.victims_group: relief workers and civilians dependent on relief
breakdown.notes: TPLF captured relief workers in 1983 and 1988 and attacked relief-related sites/routes, but HRW reports casualties from TPLF mines/attacks were infrequent and no aggregate death toll is given.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: HRW documents abuses but states that TPLF/EPRDF abuses were on a much smaller scale than government abuses. No comprehensive direct-civilian-death total was found.

sides.name: OLF and other non-EPLF / non-EPRDF insurgents
members: Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Party rural insurgents

military_killed.notes: PRIF/UCDP gives 1,484 deaths for the OLF-government war, including 364 one-sided government-violence victims, but does not provide OLF combatant deaths. Other groups are even less well quantified.

breakdown.victims_group: border-area civilians affected by WSLF and Somali-aligned forces
breakdown.notes: HRW reports killing, looting and rape by WSLF from 1978 onward but gives no consolidated death count in the consulted material.

breakdown.cause: raids, kidnapping, attacks on settlements or projects
breakdown.notes: EPRP actions in Metekel included harassment of settlements, stealing food, kidnapping three Italian workers, destroying vehicles and a water point; no civilian death total was found.
HRW notes that the OLF attacked a Sudanese refugee camp in 1990, but no death total was found in the consulted sources.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Real abuses are documented, but the available sources do not permit a responsible aggregate death toll.

totals.notes: Conservative component roll-up: at least 100,000 soldiers; at least 150,000 direct civilian killings; 400,000-590,000 famine deaths; 50,000-100,000 resettlement deaths, producing roughly 700,000-940,000. The JSON grand_high uses 2,000,000 to reflect PRIF-cited broader secondary estimates of 1-2 million, but those figures are not decomposed by side or death category.

key_atrocities.name: 1983-85 Ethiopian famine
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Derg / PDRE government, through counterinsurgency and food-control policies; drought and structural poverty also contributed
key_atrocities.notes: HRW/Africa estimated total famine deaths at 400,000-590,000 and attributed 225,000-317,000 to government violations.

key_atrocities.name: Forced resettlement program
key_atrocities.victims_group: forcibly relocated peasants and famine victims
key_atrocities.notes: About 600,000 people were forcibly relocated; death estimates range from at least 50,000 to perhaps 100,000.

key_atrocities.name: Red Terror
key_atrocities.victims_group: suspected EPRP/MEISON members, students, academics, political opponents
key_atrocities.notes: At least 10,000 killed in Addis Ababa alone; HRW later described over 150,000 killed during the Red Terror campaign.

key_atrocities.name: Ogaden counterinsurgency civilian killings
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Derg / Ethiopian army and air force
key_atrocities.notes: Civilian casualties in the Ogaden alone during the year after the Somali defeat were estimated at 25,000.

key_atrocities.name: Hawzen/Hausien bombing
key_atrocities.notes: HRW sources give 1,800 to about 2,500 civilian deaths.

key_atrocities.name: Sheib massacre and nearby killings
key_atrocities.notes: About 80 civilians were crushed by tanks and 320 killed by gunfire at Sheib; at least 100 more were killed in nearby villages.

key_atrocities.name: Afabet battle
key_atrocities.notes: Military event: at least 18,000 government troops killed or captured in two days according to OnWar/country-study summary.

uncertainty_notes: The principal uncertainties are side-specific combatant deaths, the famine counterfactual, and aggregate rebel-caused civilian deaths. UCDP/PRIF dyad totals are useful but do not split deaths by side or combatant/civilian status. HRW/Africa provides the best decomposed estimates for government direct killing, famine attribution, and resettlement, but broader 1-2 million total estimates are not decomposed enough to assign to JSON buckets without caution.

sources.title: Evil Days: 30 Years of War and Famine in Ethiopia
Evil Days: 30 Years of War and Famine in Ethiopia (PDF)
Evil Days, Chapter 9: Economic War on the Peasants and Famine
A Rare Victory for Ethiopia's Victims
